title: Refine Metric Linking — Directional Relationships and Inverse Display
story: As a data analyst, I want metric links to show directional relationships with inverse labels (e.g., 'segment of' / 'segmented by'), so that I can understand both sides of a metric relationship from either metric's perspective.
description:
Refines the metric linking feature to support directional, parent-child relationships with inverse relationship labels. When a link is created from Metric A to Metric B as 'segment of', viewing Metric B shows the inverse: 'segmented by Metric A'.

Relationship types (each has an active and inverse form):
- included in calculation of / includes in the calculation
- segment of / segmented by
- directly impacts / impacted by

The relationship sentence is concatenated for display (e.g., 'Metric 3 segment of Metric 1'). Tooltip on hover shows the full sentence. An info tooltip provides help text about metric linking.

Also fixes a bug where newly added metrics did not appear in the linking dropdown until a page refresh.
acceptance_criteria: Three directional relationship types are available: included in calculation of, segment of, directly impacts
Viewing the linked metric shows the inverse relationship label
Relationship display shows the concatenated sentence (e.g., 'Metric 3 segment of Metric 1')
Newly added metrics appear in the dropdown without requiring a page refresh
Info tooltip explains metric linking purpose
ux_changes: Relationship labels show directional sentences. Inverse relationships auto-display on the linked metric. Dropdown refresh fix for newly added metrics.
middleware_changes: Bidirectional relationship query — fetching links where the metric is either parent or child and applying the correct label direction.
data_model_changes: Relationship types updated to three with inverse labels stored in the relationship type table.
api_changes: Linked metrics endpoint returns both direct and inverse relationships with appropriate labels.
implementation_notes: Relationships are stored once (parent → child) but queried from both directions. The display logic determines which label to show based on whether the current metric is the parent or child in the relationship.
comment_summary: Developer fixed the dropdown refresh bug, implemented inverse relationship display, and verified bidirectional viewing. Approved after review.
